Callback type which represents the handler for interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.

briefdescription.para: Callback type which represents a custom timer wait handler.

detaileddescription.para: This is only used for Microblaze since it doesn't have a native sleep function. To avoid dependency on a hardware timer, the default wait functionality is implemented using loop iterations; this isn't too accurate. If a custom timer handler is used, the user may implement their own wait implementation using a hardware timer (see example/) for better accuracy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.
is the number of microseconds to be passed to the timer function.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the configuration for this Video PHY instance and fills in the InstancePtr->Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function will obtian the IP version.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 core instance.

simplesect.para: The IP version of the Video PHY core.

briefdescription.para: This function is the delay/sleep function for the XHdmiphy1 driver.

detaileddescription.para: For the Zynq family, there exists native sleep functionality. For MicroBlaze however, there does not exist such functionality. In the MicroBlaze case, the default method for delaying is to use a predetermined amount of loop iterations. This method is prone to inaccuracy and dependent on system configuration; for greater accuracy, the user may supply their own delay/sleep handler, pointed to by InstancePtr->UserTimerWaitUs, which may have better accuracy if a hardware timer is used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.
is the number of microseconds to delay/sleep for.

briefdescription.para: This function installs a callback function for the HDMIPHY error conditions.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.
CallbackFunc
is the address to the callback function.
CallbackRef
is the user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.
None.
The XHdmiphy1_ErrorHandler API calls the registered function in ErrorCallback and passes two arguments: 1) CallbackRef 2) Error Type as defined by XHdmiphy1_ErrType. Sample Function Call: CallbackFunc(CallbackRef, XHdmiphy1_ErrType);

specifies the total number of pixels per clock.
1 = XVIDC_PPC_1
2 = XVIDC_PPC_2
4 = XVIDC_PPC_4

specifies the color depth/bits per color component.
6 = XVIDC_BPC_6
8 = XVIDC_BPC_8

12 = XVIDC_BPC_12
16 = XVIDC_BPC_16

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if calculated PLL parameters updated successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the raw (unconditional) frequency of the RX/TX reference clock as measured by the clock detector peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike XHdmiphy1_ClkDetGetRefClkFreqHz, this function always returns the measured value regardless of lock status - useful for debugging.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 core instance.
is an indicator for RX or TX.

simplesect.para: The raw measured frequency of the RX/TX reference clock.
This function returns the actual measured frequency value even when lock conditions are not met. Use this for debugging to determine if a clock is present vs not locked.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the frequency of the DRU reference clock as measured by the clock detector peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 core instance.

simplesect.para: The measured frequency of the DRU reference clock.
The design must have a DRU for this function to return a valid value.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the raw (unconditional) frequency of the DRU reference clock as measured by the clock detector peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike XHdmiphy1_DruGetRefClkFreqHz, this function always returns the measured value without any range checking - useful for debugging.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 core instance.

simplesect.para: The raw measured frequency of the DRU reference clock.
This function returns the actual measured frequency value without range validation. Use this for debugging to see the raw DRU clock measurement independent of expected frequency ranges.

memberdef.name: XHdmiphy1_PllCalculator

briefdescription.para: This function will try to find the necessary PLL divisor values to produce the configured line rate given the specified PLL input frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 core instance.
is the GT quad ID to calculate the PLL values for.
is the channel ID to calculate the PLL values for.
is an indicator for TX or RX.

is the PLL input frequency on which to base the calculations on. A value of 0 indicates to use the currently configured quad PLL reference clock. A non-zero value indicates to ignore what is currently configured in SW, and use a custom frequency instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if valid PLL values were found to satisfy the constraints.

simplesect.para: If successful, the channel's PllParams structure will be modified with the valid PLL parameters.

is the channel ID to calculate the PLL values for.
is an indicator for TX or RX.

is the PLL input frequency on which to base the calculations on. A value of 0 indicates to use the currently configured quad PLL reference clock. A non-zero value indicates to ignore what is currently configured in SW, and use a custom frequency instead.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if valid PLL values were found to satisfy the constraints.

simplesect.para: If successful, the channel's PllParams structure will be modified with the valid PLL parameters.

is the GT quad ID to operate on.
is the channel ID for which to write the settings for.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the configuration was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function will check the status of a PLL lock on the specified channel.

is the GT quad ID to operate on.
is the channel ID to power down the PLL for.
is an indicator whether to "hold" the power down if set to 1. If set to 0: power down, then power back up.

briefdescription.para: This function installs a callback function for the specified handler type.

is the interrupt handler type which specifies which interrupt event to attach the callback for.
is the address to the callback function.

briefdescription.para: This function disables interrupts associated with the specified interrupt type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiphy1 instance.
is the interrupt type/mask to disable.

briefdescription.para: Check if channel ID is a regular channel.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the given channel ID represents a regular channel (channel A or channels 1-4).

parameterdescription.para: Channel ID to check.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if the ID is a regular channel, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if channel ID is a common channel.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the given channel ID represents a common channel (common channel A or common channels 0-1).

parameterdescription.para: Channel ID to check.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if the ID is a common channel, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if channel ID is the TX MMCM channel.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the given channel ID represents the transmit MMCM channel.

parameterdescription.para: Channel ID to check.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if the ID is the TX MMCM channel, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if channel ID is the RX MMCM channel.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the given channel ID represents the receive MMCM channel.

parameterdescription.para: Channel ID to check.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if the ID is the RX MMCM channel, 0 otherwise.

memberdef.name: XHdmiphy1_IsTxUsingQpll
memberdef.initializer: ((XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_TX, ChId)) || \ (XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL0 == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_TX, ChId)) || \ (XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL1 == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_TX, ChId)))

briefdescription.para: Check if TX is using QPLL.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the transmit channel is using any QPLL type (QPLL, QPLL0, or QPLL1) for the specified channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the HDMI PHY instance.
Quad ID.
Channel ID.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if TX is using QPLL, 0 otherwise.

memberdef.name: XHdmiphy1_IsRxUsingQpll
memberdef.initializer: ((XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_RX, ChId)) || \ (XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL0 == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_RX, ChId)) || \ (XHDMIPHY1_PLL_TYPE_QPLL1 == \ XHdmiphy1_GetPllType(InstancePtr, QuadId, XHDMIPHY1_DIR_RX, ChId)))

briefdescription.para: Check if RX is using QPLL.

detaileddescription.para: This macro checks if the receive channel is using any QPLL type (QPLL, QPLL0, or QPLL1) for the specified channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the HDMI PHY instance.
Quad ID.
Channel ID.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if RX is using QPLL, 0 otherwise.

Channel ID.

simplesect.para: Non-zero if RX is using CPLL, 0 otherwise.
